_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Keep-Alive Scheduler logs status=success for silent no-op CLI fires; stdout/stderr/elapsed never captured

steps.description: Database: generate an Alembic revision adding four nullable columns to keep_alive_runs (stdout TEXT, stderr TEXT, elapsed_ms INTEGER, returncode INTEGER) and extend the KeepAliveRun ORM model in orch/db/models.py with matching Mapped[...] declarations. Strip every autogenerate noise line that isn't an add_column on keep_alive_runs. Downgrade drops the four columns in reverse order. All four columns must be nullable so existing rows survive without a backfill. Must run `make migration-check` and report PASS. Do NOT touch orch/keep_alive_service.py, the poller, templates, or test files — those are S03/S05/S07 scope.

Per-agent review of S01 (Database): migration adds exactly four add_column entries (no autogenerate noise); downgrade drops them in reverse order; ORM Mapped[] declarations exactly match the migration column names and types; down_revision points at a real head; files_changed contains exactly two paths (the revision file and orch/db/models.py); `make migration-check` reproduces PASS.

Backend: introduce frozen dataclass FireResult(returncode, stdout, stderr, elapsed_ms) with property is_success encoding the I-00112 contract (rc==0 AND stdout.strip()!='' AND elapsed_ms >= _MIN_SUCCESS_ELAPSED_MS where _MIN_SUCCESS_ELAPSED_MS=500 is a module-level constant). Refactor fire_claude to return FireResult; capture elapsed_ms via time.perf_counter inside both the try block and every except branch. Extend keep_alive_service.log_run to accept and persist keyword-only stdout/stderr/elapsed_ms/returncode. Refactor KeepAlivePoller._fire_slot to consult result.is_success (NOT result.returncode) and _log_run to persist the FireResult on every run. Log line carries rc / elapsed_ms / stdout_len (NEVER raw stdout). Retry policy unchanged — silent no-op also triggers the single retry. Do NOT touch the migration, the model, any template, or any test file. Existing tests broken by the contract change ARE the RED evidence — record the failure in tdd_red_evidence; S07 will rewrite them.

Per-agent review of S03 (Backend): FireResult.is_success applies all three conditions exactly (rc==0 AND stdout.strip()!='' AND elapsed_ms >= _MIN_SUCCESS_ELAPSED_MS); the 500ms floor exists as a single named constant (no inline magic number); _fire_slot consults result.is_success (returncode check is the bug class returning); persistence covers every code path (success AND failure); time.perf_counter (not time.time) is used; elapsed captured in every except branch; log line carries rc/elapsed_ms/stdout_len, NEVER raw stdout; %-format style for logger (not f-string); scope adherence (orch/keep_alive_service.py + orch/daemon/keep_alive_poller.py only).

Frontend: extend dashboard/templates/fragments/keep_alive_runs.html with two new columns AFTER Status: Elapsed ({{ run.elapsed_ms }} ms, `—` when NULL — use `is not none` NOT bare truthy because 0 ms is legitimate) and Output (first 80 chars of run.stdout with full string HTML-escaped in title attribute via `|e`, `—` when NULL). Verify get_recent_runs already passes ORM objects to the template (likely no router change required). Touch _partials/help/keep_alive.html only if it currently documents columns. Regenerate dashboard/static/styles.css via `make css` only if new Tailwind utility classes were introduced (verify with grep first); if `make css` is no-op or fails, append plain CSS rules directly to styles.css. Do NOT touch orch/, models, migrations, or any test file.

Per-agent review of S05 (Frontend): fragment has exactly five column headers (Fired At / Slot / Status / Elapsed / Output) in that order; NULL guard uses `is not none` for elapsed_ms (NOT truthy — 0 ms is legitimate); run.stdout is HTML-escaped in title attribute (`|e`) — missing escape is CRITICAL XSS; fragment does NOT extend base.html; no dynamic class construction; %-style format-filter (NOT {} per I-00075); styles.css touched only if a new Tailwind class was introduced; scope adherence (no orch/, model, or test files touched).

Tests: (a) CREATE tests/unit/test_keep_alive_poller_success_contract.py with the six tests verbatim from the design's `## Test to Reproduce` section. ALL tests MUST mock at orch.keep_alive_service.subprocess.run (NOT at fire_claude — mocking at the wrapper re-introduces the bug class's blind spot). Mock time.perf_counter with deterministic side_effect pairs. Tests 5 and 6 also need a KeepAliveSlot row inserted via the project's testcontainer DB fixture. (b) UPDATE tests/unit/test_keep_alive_service.py and tests/unit/test_keep_alive_poller.py to match S03's FireResult return shape — rewrite assertions to check result.is_success and result.returncode/stdout/stderr/elapsed_ms; DO NOT silently delete real coverage. (c) CREATE/EXTEND tests/dashboard/test_keep_alive_runs_table.py with two render tests: NULL-fallback (legacy row → `—` in Elapsed and Output) and populated (3500 ms / 'OK reply' snippet / title attribute carries full stdout). Tests live under tests/dashboard/ (the `client` fixture is registered only there per I-00067). Every assertion must be SEMANTIC — specific cell content like `>3500 ms<`, `title="OK reply"`, status in ('failed','retried_failed') — never shape-only. Targeted verification only: `uv run pytest tests/unit/test_keep_alive_poller_success_contract.py tests/unit/test_keep_alive_service.py tests/unit/test_keep_alive_poller.py tests/dashboard/test_keep_alive_runs_table.py -v`. Do NOT run make test-unit / make test-integration / make test-frontend / make allure-integration. Do NOT touch any production code.

Per-agent review of S07 (Tests): all six tests from `## Test to Reproduce` are present and named exactly; success-contract tests mock at subprocess.run (NOT fire_claude); time.perf_counter is mocked for determinism; dashboard render tests live under tests/dashboard/ (NOT unit or integration — I-00067); assertions are semantic (specific values + structured locations) never shape-only; deleted tests (if any) are justified in S07's notes; no production code touched; pytest-randomly compatibility (order-independent).

Global cross-agent review of S01..S07: AC1 (silent no-op classified failed) demonstrated by test_i00112_poller_logs_failed_when_contract_violated + poller consults is_success; AC2 (real round-trip classified success) demonstrated by test_i00112_real_round_trip_is_success; AC3 (regression tests exist) by all six tests green; AC4 (dashboard shows new columns) by template + tests/dashboard/test_keep_alive_runs_table.py; AC5 (migration round-trips) by make migration-check. Cross-layer schema/ORM/runtime consistency. Template field names exist on the model. `_MIN_SUCCESS_ELAPSED_MS = 500` exists exactly once (no duplicate magic numbers). Contract enforced in exactly one place (FireResult.is_success); poller does NOT re-derive it. Functional doc accurately predicts user-observable changes. Scope adherence (no file outside scope.allowed_paths). XSS protection on stdout title attribute.
QV: Alembic migration round-trip + drift check

QV: Browser verification — verify the Recent Executions table on /system/keep-alive has the new Elapsed and Output columns; legacy NULL rows render `—` without crashing; freshly fired (or seeded) populated rows show elapsed_ms and stdout snippet plus a full-stdout title attribute; no regressions on adjacent flows.
Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill
